title: Dataset: EEG-Controlled Exoskeleton for Walking and Standing - A Longitudinal Study of Healthy Individuals

funding_references.funder_name: UH–Methodist Graduate Fellowship for Translational Research

description: This longitudinal brain-machine interface dataset comprises multimodal recordings from seven healthy adults performing motor imagery tasks to control a lower-limb exoskeleton. The dataset includes 60-channel EEG, EOG, dual IMU sensors, and exoskeleton control signals across nine sessions per participant, combining open-loop calibration and closed-loop BMI control paradigms for walking and standing motor imagery.
methods_description: Data collection involved seven healthy adult participants (ages 20–30 years) completing nine sessions each. Each session consisted of a motor imagery training phase for BMI decoder calibration, followed by twelve closed-loop trials organized into three blocks, and two extended motor imagery tasks (6-minute walk and stop imagery). Recordings included 60-channel scalp EEG with 4-channel EOG, 3-axis IMU data (accelerometer, gyroscope, magnetometer, quaternion) from forehead and exoskeleton-mounted sensors, and real-time BMI control and feedback signals.
